# Failure-ratio scoping of brute-force offenders via dataset self-join (T1110)

**Dataset**: `gx_05_auth`

Scopes brute-force suspects by computing what fraction of each offending user's total authentication activity was failures - a high ratio means the account is almost entirely failed attempts (attacker), a low ratio means a few failures buried in normal use (likely fat-fingering). It first reduces to users with >= 3 failures, then `join type=inner` against a second pass over the same dataset that totals all events and distinct source IPs per user. The fail ratio needs `divide(to_float(failures), to_float(total_events))` - wrapping both operands in `to_float` avoids truncating integer division.

```sql
dataset = gx_05_auth
| filter outcome = "failure"
| comp count() as failures by user
| filter failures >= 3
| join type=inner (dataset = gx_05_auth | comp count() as total_events, count_distinct(src_ip) as distinct_ips by user) as t t.user = user
| alter fail_ratio = divide(to_float(failures), to_float(total_events))
| sort desc failures
```
